report_name: 'The Gentlemen' Ransomware Deploys Self-Propagating Malware in Global RaaS Campaign

threat_actors.name: The Gentlemen
threat_actors.description: A financially motivated cybercrime operation, tracked by Microsoft as Storm-2697, that began as a private group in mid-2025 and transitioned to a Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) platform. They developed and operate the Go-based 'The Gentlemen' ransomware and recruit affiliates via forums like BreachForums.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through a double-extortion ransomware model, targeting critical sectors worldwide.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exfiltrate sensitive data before encryption as part of a double-extortion model.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The Gentlemen ransomware encrypts files on the victim's network using a combination of Curve25519 and XChaCha20.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: The malware terminates processes associated with security software and backup solutions before propagation and encryption, using commands like 'taskkill /F /IM <security_tool>.exe'.

attack_patterns.name: Clear Windows Event Logs
attack_patterns.description: The malware clears system and security event logs using 'wevtutil.exe cl' to hide its tracks.

attack_patterns.name: SMB/Windows Admin Shares
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware's self-propagation module uses stolen or harvested credentials to access network shares for lateral movement.

attack_patterns.name: Windows Management Instrumentation
attack_patterns.description: WMI is used as a method for lateral movement and deploying the ransomware payload across the network.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The malware deletes volume shadow copies using 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows /all /quiet' to prevent system recovery.

malware.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware
malware.description: A Go-based ransomware payload featuring strong encryption (Curve25519 and XChaCha20) and an aggressive self-propagation module for automated lateral movement. It is obfuscated with the Garble tool.

tools.name: Garble
tools.description: A Go obfuscator used to protect The Gentlemen ransomware from reverse engineering.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Mentioned as a potential tool used for deploying the ransomware during lateral movement.

tools.name: WMI
tools.description: Mentioned as a potential tool used for deploying the ransomware payload during lateral movement.

identities.name: BreachForums
identities.description: A criminal marketplace where The Gentlemen ransomware group recruits affiliates.

identities.name: Healthcare Sector
identities.description: A critical sector targeted by The Gentlemen ransomware group.

identities.name: Financial Sector
identities.description: A critical sector targeted by The Gentlemen ransomware group.

identities.name: Transportation Sector
identities.description: A critical sector targeted by The Gentlemen ransomware group.

identities.name: Education Sector
identities.description: A sector targeted by The Gentlemen ransomware group.

indicators.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware Security Tool Termination
indicators.pattern:
title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Security Tool Termination
id: 2b1f8c9a-0d3e-4f5a-b1c2-d3e4f5a6b7c8
status: experimental
description: Detects command-line activity aimed at terminating security software, a TTP used by The Gentlemen ransomware.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/ransomware/the-gentlemen-ransomware-combines-advanced-encryption-with-self-propagation-targeting-critical-sectors/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1562.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\taskkill.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '/f'
      - '/im'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of 'taskkill.exe' with parameters to forcefully terminate processes, a technique used by The Gentlemen ransomware to disable security tools.

indicators.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware Event Log Clearing
indicators.pattern:
title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Event Log Clearing
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-3a2b-1c0d-ef9876543210
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wevtutil.exe to clear Windows Event Logs, a TTP used by The Gentlemen ransomware to cover its tracks.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/ransomware/the-gentlemen-ransomware-combines-advanced-encryption-with-self-propagation-targeting-critical-sectors/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1070.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wevtutil.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' cl '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of 'wevtutil.exe' to clear event logs, a defense evasion technique used by The Gentlemen ransomware.

indicators.name: The Gentlemen Ransomware Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 5d6e7f8a-9b0c-1d2e-3f4a-5b6c7d8e9f0a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware precursor technique also associated with The Gentlemen.
references:
  - https://industrialcyber.co/ransomware/the-gentlemen-ransomware-combines-advanced-encryption-with-self-propagation-targeting-critical-sectors/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-01
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of 'vssadmin.exe' to delete volume shadow copies, a technique to inhibit system recovery used by ransomware like The Gentlemen.
